In her year-long journey through the California justice system, Katherine, a 35-year-old single mother with three kids, faced setbacks at every corner. After leaving her abusive husband, she moved into the only apartment she could afford, only to find a broken toilet and a non-working oven, rats and roaches, and a fourth-floor landing with no railing. She started holding back rent until her landlord would fix things, but then her Medi-Cal benefits got cut off cause she couldn't show rent receipts. She lost health care for her kids and herself, even though she needs medicine for her borderline diabetes and her kids were getting sick from rat bites. Katherine tried to get help through the courts. Representing herself at an administrative hearing, she lost her appeal to get Medi-Cal back cause she didn’t have the right rent documents. When she went to the courthouse to complain about her landlord, the process was too confusing, so she just went back home. According to "The Path to Justice: Why did Katherine rep herself instead of having someone else rep her?
text_b: She couldn't afford no private lawyer and public resources be scarce.
